physics.title: Physics BSc / MPhys
physics.overview: Physics is the most fundamental of the natural sciences, seeking to understand the laws that govern everything from subatomic particles to the structure of the universe. A UK physics degree combines rigorous mathematical training with laboratory work, computational skills, and deep conceptual understanding. The MPhys (4-year integrated Masters) is strongly recommended for students considering research careers or competitive graduate programmes.

professionalBodies.name: Institute of Physics (IoP)
professionalBodies.benefit: Accredited degrees meet IoP requirements for Chartered Physicist (CPhys). IoP membership provides networking, careers support and access to journals.

professionalBodies.name: European Physical Society
professionalBodies.benefit: Pan-European recognition for physics qualifications and access to conferences and research collaborations.

physics.employerPerspective: Physics graduates are among the most sought-after recruits in quantitative finance, technology and defence — industries that value the ability to build and solve complex mathematical models. Goldman Sachs, McKinsey and ARM specifically target physics MPhys graduates for their quantitative roles. The MPhys degree signals an ability to complete an independent research project, which is highly valued.
physics.entryRequirements: Typically A*AA–ABB at A-level. Mathematics A-level is always required; Physics A-level is required at all universities. Further Mathematics is strongly recommended at top departments (Oxford and Cambridge require it effectively). Most universities also accept the IB with 36–38 points including 6/7 in HL Physics and HL Maths.
physics.furtherStudy: PhD in physics, astrophysics, materials science, biophysics or related fields. MSc in Data Science, Quantitative Finance (MFE), or Machine Learning. Part III Mathematical Tripos at Cambridge for those wanting to bridge to mathematics.
isItForYou: You love mathematics and want to apply it to understand fundamental reality
You enjoy both theory and hands-on lab experiments
You want maximum flexibility — physics opens doors to tech, finance, research and engineering
You are comfortable with ambiguity and open-ended problems
Note: Physics is mathematically demanding. If you prefer computer science or statistics to differential equations, consider Data Science or Maths & CS instead

astrophysics.title: Astrophysics BSc / MPhys
astrophysics.overview: Astrophysics combines the fundamental principles of physics with the study of stars, galaxies, black holes, dark matter and the evolution of the universe. UK departments are world-class — the discovery of pulsars (Cambridge), gravitational wave detection (Cardiff/Glasgow/Birmingham LIGO group) and X-ray astronomy (Leicester) were all made here. A degree in astrophysics produces physicists with strong programming and data analysis skills highly prized across science and industry.

professionalBodies.name: Institute of Physics (IoP)
professionalBodies.benefit: IoP accreditation for Chartered Physicist status and access to careers and networking events.

professionalBodies.name: Royal Astronomical Society (RAS)
professionalBodies.benefit: Free student membership available; access to journals, meetings and a strong professional community.

astrophysics.employerPerspective: The data science and programming skills developed in astrophysics are increasingly valued outside academia. Former astrophysicists are prominent at Google DeepMind, in financial modelling, and in the growing UK space sector. The analytical rigour of an astrophysics degree is highly transferable.
astrophysics.entryRequirements: Typically A*AA–BCC at A-level depending on institution. Mathematics and Physics A-levels required at all institutions. Some programmes (e.g. Durham, UCL) additionally recommend Further Mathematics. Accessible entry routes exist at Keele and Hertfordshire.
astrophysics.furtherStudy: PhD in astrophysics, cosmology, space science or gravitational wave physics. MSc in Data Science, Space Science or AI. Industry-funded PhD studentships at Airbus, ESA and STFC partner institutions.
isItForYou: You are fascinated by the universe, black holes, dark matter or the origins of the cosmos
You enjoy physics but want it anchored in real observed phenomena
You want strong programming and data analysis skills built into your degree
You are comfortable studying away from major cities (many top astrophysics departments are in Durham, St Andrews or Nottingham)
Note: Astrophysics has fewer university options than straight Physics; competition for PhD places is intense

theoreticalPhysics.title: Theoretical Physics MPhys
theoreticalPhysics.overview: Theoretical Physics is the most mathematically rigorous and intellectually demanding physics degree in the UK. It focuses on the mathematical frameworks that underpin physical reality: quantum field theory, general relativity, string theory, condensed matter theory and statistical mechanics. Graduates from the best programmes are among the most analytically capable graduates in any discipline.
skills: Mathematical modelling at the deepest level

professionalBodies.name: Institute of Physics (IoP)
professionalBodies.benefit: Accredited for Chartered Physicist; IoP Theory Group membership connects to the research community.

theoreticalPhysics.employerPerspective: Theoretical physics graduates are in exceptional demand in quantitative finance — Jane Street, Citadel and Millennium Partners pay extremely high salaries to recruit them. The degree signals mastery of abstract mathematics and the ability to build and solve complex models, which is the core skill in derivatives pricing and systematic trading.
theoreticalPhysics.entryRequirements: Typically A*A*A–AAA at A-level. Mathematics at A* and Physics A-level both required. Further Mathematics strongly recommended and effectively required at Oxford, Cambridge and Imperial. Competitive entrance tests (MAT, PAT, NSAA) at Oxbridge.
theoreticalPhysics.furtherStudy: PhD in theoretical physics, mathematical physics or cosmology. MSc in Mathematical and Theoretical Physics (Oxford) or Part III Mathematical Tripos (Cambridge). MSc in Quantitative Finance (MFE). Research positions at CERN, IPPP or Perimeter Institute.
isItForYou: You prefer abstract mathematical reasoning to experimental work
You want to understand the deepest theoretical foundations of physics
You are comfortable with very high mathematical demand from Year 1
You are targeting research, quantitative finance or top-tier tech
Note: Theoretical physics requires strong A* in Mathematics — if your mathematical strength is more moderate, standard Physics MPhys is the better route

mathematicalPhysics.title: Mathematical Physics MPhys
mathematicalPhysics.overview: Mathematical Physics sits at the intersection of mathematics and physics, using rigorous mathematical structures to describe physical phenomena. Unlike theoretical physics (which is embedded in physics departments), mathematical physics programs often have joint delivery between physics and mathematics schools, giving students deep expertise in both disciplines. Graduates are exceptionally well-prepared for research in either field.

modules: Extended Research Project in Mathematical Physics

skills: Physical intuition combined with mathematical precision

professionalBodies.name: London Mathematical Society (LMS)
professionalBodies.benefit: Student membership available; access to journals and meetings across pure and applied mathematics.

mathematicalPhysics.employerPerspective: Mathematical physics graduates are rare and highly prized. The combination of mathematical rigour and physical insight makes them exceptional candidates for quantitative finance, cryptography and defence research — fields where most candidates have only one of the two skill sets.
mathematicalPhysics.entryRequirements: Typically A*AA–AAB at A-level. Both Mathematics (usually at A*) and Physics are required. Further Mathematics strongly recommended. Some programmes (Edinburgh, Glasgow) accept Scottish Highers with Advanced Highers in Mathematics and Physics.
mathematicalPhysics.furtherStudy: PhD in mathematical physics, differential geometry or quantum gravity. Part III Mathematics at Cambridge. MSc Mathematical Physics at Edinburgh or King's. Research at Perimeter Institute or CERN Theory Division.
isItForYou: You love both pure mathematics and physics equally
You want to work on the frontiers where mathematics and physics meet
You are comfortable with proof-based mathematics from Year 1
You are considering a research career in geometry, quantum gravity or mathematical analysis
Note: Fewer universities offer this specific programme — check carefully whether your preferred university delivers it jointly between both departments

physicsPhilosophy.title: Physics and Philosophy MPhysPhil
physicsPhilosophy.overview: Physics and Philosophy is one of the most intellectually distinctive undergraduate degrees available in the UK. It combines the mathematical rigour of an MPhys with the analytical depth of philosophy — tackling questions at the very foundations of knowledge: What is time? What does quantum mechanics tell us about the nature of reality? Is mathematics discovered or invented? Oxford's MPhysPhil is the flagship example, but strong programmes exist at Durham, Bristol, Manchester and Leeds.

professionalBodies.name: Institute of Physics (IoP)
professionalBodies.benefit: IoP accreditation maintained for physics component.

professionalBodies.name: Mind Association
professionalBodies.benefit: Access to philosophy research community and publications in philosophy of mind and physics.

physicsPhilosophy.employerPerspective: Physics and Philosophy graduates are among the most analytically versatile in the graduate market. The combination of quantitative precision and philosophical argumentation is highly attractive to law firms, management consultancies and science policy bodies. Oxford MPhysPhil graduates are disproportionately represented at McKinsey and the top law chambers.
physicsPhilosophy.entryRequirements: Typically A*A*A–AAA. Oxford requires MAT plus interview. Most programmes require Mathematics and Physics at A-level; Philosophy A-level is welcome but not required. Further Mathematics strongly recommended at Oxford.
physicsPhilosophy.furtherStudy: BPhil or DPhil in Philosophy of Physics at Oxford. PhD in theoretical physics. Graduate Diploma in Law (GDL) for law conversion. MSc Philosophy of Science at LSE.
isItForYou: You want to understand physics at its deepest conceptual and philosophical level
You enjoy both rigorous mathematical argument and philosophical essay writing
You are interested in what quantum mechanics really means — not just how to calculate with it
You want a degree that is genuinely unusual and intellectually distinctive
Note: You will study substantially less experimental physics than on a pure MPhys; this degree leads less naturally to an experimental physics career

spaceScience.title: Space Science and Astronomy MPhys
spaceScience.overview: Space Science and Astronomy degrees focus on observational and experimental aspects of the universe — from exoplanet detection to gravitational wave astronomy, from space instrumentation to planetary science. The UK is a world leader in space science: Leicester built X-ray instruments on dozens of missions; UCL's Mullard Space Science Laboratory leads ESA collaborations; and Cardiff, Glasgow and Birmingham operate the UK's LIGO gravitational wave detectors.
skills: Remote sensing and satellite data analysis

professionalBodies.name: Royal Astronomical Society (RAS)
professionalBodies.benefit: Free student membership; access to journals, careers support and the RAS National Astronomy Meeting.

spaceScience.employerPerspective: The UK space sector is growing rapidly — UKSA, Airbus Stevenage and Reaction Engines are all expanding. Space science graduates with programming, instrumentation and data skills are increasingly in demand. Leicester and UCL graduates are disproportionately hired into ESA and satellite companies.
spaceScience.entryRequirements: Typically A*AA–BCC at A-level depending on university. Mathematics and Physics always required. Accessible routes exist at Keele, Hertfordshire and Kent. Top programmes (UCL, Durham) require A*AA.
spaceScience.furtherStudy: PhD in astrophysics, space science, planetary science or gravitational wave physics. MSc Space Engineering or MSc Data Science. STFC-funded PhD studentships widely available.
isItForYou: You are passionate about space exploration, telescopes and astronomical discovery
You want to combine physics with real mission engineering and observation
You are attracted to the growing commercial and public sector space industry
You enjoy both data analysis and the physics underlying it
Note: Space science has fewer university options than physics; the job market in academic astrophysics is competitive — strong programming skills open many more doors

computationalPhysics.title: Computational Physics / Physics with Data Science MPhys
computationalPhysics.overview: Computational Physics and Physics with Data Science degrees prepare students to use programming, machine learning and numerical simulation as core scientific tools. They combine the rigour of a physics degree with software engineering and data science skills that are in exceptional demand in tech, finance and scientific research. This is one of the most career-versatile physics routes available.

professionalBodies.name: Institute of Physics (IoP)
professionalBodies.benefit: IoP accreditation for Chartered Physicist; Computing in Physics group provides specialist networking.

computationalPhysics.employerPerspective: Physics with strong coding is the ideal background for machine learning research and quantitative roles. Google, NVIDIA and Palantir actively target computational physics graduates. The combination of physical intuition, mathematical rigour and programming ability is rare and extremely well-compensated.
computationalPhysics.entryRequirements: Typically A*AA–AAB at A-level. Mathematics and Physics required. Computing A-level is welcomed but not required — programming is taught from scratch. Bath and Exeter have slightly lower typical offers (AAB) than Edinburgh and Warwick (A*AA).
computationalPhysics.furtherStudy: PhD in computational physics, machine learning for science or quantum computing. MSc in Data Science, AI or Scientific Computing. Industry-sponsored PhDs at NVIDIA, ARM and Microsoft Research.
isItForYou: You want a physics degree that also builds marketable software engineering skills
You enjoy programming and see it as a powerful tool for solving physical problems
You want strong career options in tech, finance or scientific research equally
You are interested in quantum computing, machine learning or simulation
Note: This degree involves more programming and less wet lab work than standard physics — ideal if lab sessions are not your strength

medicalPhysics.title: Medical Physics MPhys
medicalPhysics.overview: Medical Physics applies the principles of physics to healthcare — from MRI and CT scanning to radiotherapy for cancer, nuclear medicine and ultrasound. The UK NHS employs around 10,000 medical physicists and the career pathway is well-defined via the IPEM (Institute of Physics and Engineering in Medicine). Surrey runs the longest-established medical physics degree in the UK; Nottingham is home to the MRI inventor's centre.
skills: Data analysis in a clinical context

professionalBodies.name: Institute of Physics and Engineering in Medicine (IPEM)
professionalBodies.benefit: Accredited degrees lead to IPEM affiliate membership and are required for the NHS Medical Physics Training Scheme entry.

medicalPhysics.employerPerspective: The NHS Medical Physics Training Scheme offers a structured, salaried, postgraduate clinical training pathway leading to IPEM Membership and Clinical Scientist registration. It is competitive but medical physics graduates from accredited programmes have a clear route to a valued, well-paid NHS career with good job security.
medicalPhysics.entryRequirements: Typically A*AA–AAB at A-level. Physics and Mathematics required; Biology or Chemistry often recommended. IPEM-accredited degrees are listed on the IPEM website. Clinical placement experience in NHS hospitals is highly valued by all programmes.
medicalPhysics.furtherStudy: NHS Medical Physics Training Scheme (postgraduate clinical training). MSc Medical Physics at Leeds, UCL or King's. PhD in medical imaging, radiotherapy physics or medical devices.
isItForYou: You want to apply physics in a healthcare context and make a direct difference to patients
You are interested in MRI, radiotherapy, nuclear medicine or imaging technology
You want a structured NHS career pathway after graduation
You are comfortable in a clinical hospital environment
Note: Medical Physics is more vocationally focused than standard physics — fewer routes into quantitative finance, tech or academic research

geophysics.title: Geophysics MPhys
geophysics.overview: Geophysics applies the methods of physics to study the Earth and other planets: seismic wave propagation, the Earth's magnetic field, plate tectonics, volcanic processes, and subsurface imaging. UK geophysics departments have strong industry links to oil, gas, mining and renewable energy. Edinburgh and Southampton have major geophysical research groups; Leeds is closely linked to the oil exploration sector; Bristol has one of the UK's top volcanology groups.

professionalBodies.name: Geological Society of London
professionalBodies.benefit: Chartered Geologist (CGeol) route; Fellowship available to graduates with industry experience.

professionalBodies.name: Society of Exploration Geophysicists (SEG)
professionalBodies.benefit: Student membership available; access to the world's largest geophysics conference and industry network.

geophysics.employerPerspective: Geophysics graduates with seismic processing skills are highly sought after by energy companies. While the oil sector remains a major employer, the transition to renewable energy is opening new roles in wind and tidal energy site assessment and carbon capture geology.
geophysics.entryRequirements: Typically A*AA–AAB at A-level. Physics and Mathematics required at all programmes. Geology or Geography at A-level is welcome but not required — Earth science is taught from scratch. Leeds and Durham have the strongest oil sector placement records.
geophysics.furtherStudy: PhD in geophysics, seismology, volcanology or climate science. MSc Applied Geophysics (widely available). NERC-funded PhD studentships and Shell/BP-funded scholarships widely available.
isItForYou: You want to apply physics to real-world problems involving the Earth and energy
You enjoy fieldwork outdoors as well as computational modelling
You want strong links to the energy industry from your degree
You are interested in climate change, natural hazards or resource exploration
Note: Oil industry recruitment is strong but cyclical; climate and renewable energy is the fastest-growing employer sector for geophysicists

physicsIndustry.title: Physics with Year in Industry MPhys
physicsIndustry.duration: 4 years (MPhys with integrated placement) or 5 years at Surrey
physicsIndustry.overview: Physics with Year in Industry combines a rigorous 4-year MPhys with a paid placement year at a UK or international employer in engineering, defence, energy, technology or research. Bath, Surrey, Warwick and Southampton have the strongest placement networks. Graduates consistently earn higher starting salaries than standard physics graduates and have a lower unemployment rate. The placement year is typically completed between Years 2 and 3 or Years 3 and 4.
skills: All standard physics MPhys skills PLUS

professionalBodies.name: Institute of Physics (IoP)
professionalBodies.benefit: IoP accreditation with additional recognition for the professional experience component.

physicsIndustry.employerPerspective: Physics placement graduates are measurably preferred by engineering and tech employers who want graduates who already understand how a professional workplace functions. Bath and Surrey placement graduates typically start at 15–20% higher salaries than non-placement physics graduates. Rolls-Royce, BAE and Dyson regularly convert placement students to permanent roles.
physicsIndustry.entryRequirements: Typically A*AA–ABB at A-level. Same entry requirements as standard Physics MPhys — the placement year does not lower or raise academic requirements. Mathematics and Physics A-levels required. Strong numeracy and communication skills assessed at placement interviews.
physicsIndustry.furtherStudy: Many graduates use their placement employer contact to secure a sponsored PhD or funded research role directly. Graduate schemes at Rolls-Royce, BAE and Leonardo are the most common destination. MSc or PhD for those wishing to return to academia.
isItForYou: You want the rigour of a physics degree combined with real-world industrial experience
You want to explore whether you prefer industry or research before committing to a PhD
You want to start earning earlier and build your professional CV before graduation
Bath and Surrey are particularly strong for this route — consider them above their overall ranking
Note: The placement year requires you to secure a position (with university support) and adds one year to the degree duration
